comment.body:
I see some constant repeated Qs about the mean reversion threshold lvls.
I will write a post about it ,but in general mean reversion refers to the tendency of a stochastic process to drift toward its long-term average level over time.
If you spend some time with reading through these very important writings, you will see what it means https://stochvoltrader.substack.com/s/educational-posts

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
so what I'm doing for you in these weeks, is basically four things:
1) researching what the market is all about.. what is the strategy, where the river flows...
2) having a probability distirbution for certain ranges
3) read the MM positioning to project possible dynamics that we should see in an ideal world (any deviation from this reveals the hands of the underlying customer interest)
4) read the vol surface and project lvls where the underlying vol dynamics are expected to shift, and looking for deviations
+1) constantly monitoring how the sentiment is evolving, constantly comparing it to the underlying driver of the markets (the first point)

having all of these, we can have a bias, and we can build a strategy.
more nuanced pictures, including non linear models should be used only when you manage a complex portfolio and you want o to constatnly optimalize its sortino

comment.body: I had a very green week, trading mean reversions mainly to the long side. Using the 10m momentum indicator for entries. comment.body: Alma,  from following Adam Mancini, his focus is Es.  The market develops magnetic levels of price over time.  The levels become points of support and resistance. The reference to magnetic means to me that it is a mean level that price expands an reverts to multiple times.  As price moves up or down out of the standard range of the magnet level it will develop a new magnet area that is now the new mean reversion level.   I am not a math expert.  Based on the observation.  Could you develop a model that identifies the magnet price/ level after it has presented itself to show the standard deviations from the new magnet price level.  The magnet price can be the turning point as much as 10 or more time over a period of days, an can return to the area after it has moved away from it as the markets move up and down.

comment.body:
Hi Alma! It was a decent week for me, caught most of the moves intraday, until yesterday I blew up using 0DTEs with a short bias(short on some tech stocks and bitcoin related). I think a lot of problems are on me as many of bad habits occur when that squeeze happened. My major question would be how you determine stop-loss on your options entry with theta burning(premium will decay even when stock price is just dancing on ur  level)?
For me I need to cool down and leave the market for a few days, reflecting on this pitfall as I really do want this be the last one. Also got a interview next week so gotta prepare for that:))

comment.body: Alma, I have quant question regarding calc of SPX/LTY correlation.  I am tracking a daily rolling correlation in Excel and am curious how you calculate this:  1) do you use TNX (10y)? or longer duration for LTY? 2) should correlation be based on raw numbers or returns (it seems using returns is more robust but I'm surprised at difference in results), 3) which trailing period do you prefer for say a daily rolling correlation?  I'm currently using 15 periods.  Thanks so much!
comment.raw_body: Alma, I have quant question regarding calc of SPX/LTY correlation.  I am tracking a daily rolling correlation in Excel and am curious how you calculate this:  1) do you use TNX (10y)? or longer duration for LTY? 2) should correlation be based on raw numbers or returns (it seems using returns is more robust but I'm surprised at difference in results), 3) which trailing period do you prefer for say a daily rolling correlation?  I'm currently using 15 periods.  Thanks so much!

comment.body: Why do you use expected realized vol? Have you tried IV? Basically just take ATM IV, divide by 16 and then you have your daily move from yesterdays close. Usually good enough

comment.body:
I didn’t get time to do monitor market constantly due to work/meetings…so I took a more conservative approach ,when I get reversion signals, I do credit spread with low deltas… usually at you 95% lvls… and then I just wait for the options to decay away in the day and it has worked well for the past 2 weeks for me…

Physiologically, I was able to do larger positions with this approach vs buying call/puts directly too

comment.body:
I can’t imagine Putin likes being Xi’s bitch but he knows US changes it’s mind every 4yrs so it’s an unreliable ally. Also he is determined to rebuild the Russian Empire, which is completely unrealistic he has an economy the size of Italy and military hardware which is outdated. A population in decline, as I understand it almost 1 million men in their prime dead or wounded and 1.5 million more left and are not coming back. 

His war has been a disaster for Russia, his only hope is that Trump allows him out with some dignity, Trump will hoping to split up the alliance between Russia and China but I think Trump is daft. 

It’s the old scorpion and the frog story. Scorpion ask’s frog to give him a lift across the river, at first frog says no because scorpion is dangerous but scorpion talks him into it as scorpion says it’s in his interest that frog survives as he will be on frogs back.  Half way across the river the scorpion sting the frog, as they start to drown the frog says why, we will both die. The scorpion shrugs and say’s It’s what I do.

Putin is a scorpion and will stab West in the back as soon as it suits him

This weekend i will head to the casino to watch March Madness a college basketball tournament. i don't watch much college basketball so if anyone got tips for possible bets it would be greatly appreciated hahaha.

comment.body:
Hi Alma! I had a nice green week. 💰

Your "supportive" stance influenced me to decide to NOT TRADE OPTIONS this week, just bull commons, leveraged etfs, etc. It also led me to end flat almost everyday which was so crucial with the lower opens. So thank you!!! 🙌🏼

Weekend plans -> spend time with my wife and three sons! Play, clean, watch sports, have friends over, go to church service tomorrow morning, and enjoy life! Hope you have a wonderful weekend! All the best to you Alma! ❤️🙂
